title: Bread and butter pickles
subtitle: Sweet-sour sliced cucumber pickles from the American South.
excerpt: Cucumber slices brined in salt overnight, then pickled in a sweet, turmeric-yellow brine of cider vinegar, sugar and mustard seeds. The classic American pickle for burgers and sandwiches. Makes about 3 jars.
sourceNotes: Bread and butter pickles. The term 'bread and butter pickle' is documented in the American South from the early 20th century, popularised commercially by Omar and Cora Fanning in 1923. The name is thought to derive from their role as an inexpensive sandwich filling during lean times. The recipe appears in American home canning manuals throughout the 20th century.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to eat after 24 hours; best after 3 days. Keeps refrigerated for 2 months.

content.text: The salting step is not optional: it draws moisture from the cucumbers so the brine is not diluted. Salt overnight for firm, properly pickled slices. A quick hour in salt gives a softer, less stable result. The turmeric gives the brine its characteristic yellow colour and faint bitterness; the sugar balances the vinegar.

Toss {{cucumber}} of sliced cucumber and {{onion}} of sliced onion with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt in a large bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight. Drain and rinse well under cold water; pat dry.
Make the brine and pack the jars

Combine {{cider-vinegar}} of cider vinegar, {{granulated-sugar}} of sugar, {{mustard-seeds-yellow}} of mustard seeds and {{turmeric}} of turmeric in a saucepan. Bring to the boil, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Pack the drained cucumber and onion tightly into sterilised jars. Pour the hot brine over the vegetables, making sure they are fully submerged. Seal immediately. Leave for at least 24 hours before eating, refrigerated.

items.cause: Cucumbers not salted long enough, or brine poured too cold.
items.fix: Salt for a full 8 hours. Pour the brine at a rolling boil; this sets the texture.

items.cause: Not enough sugar, or jar opened before 24 hours.
items.fix: The sweet-sour balance needs time. A jar tasted within hours of making will taste raw and sharp.
Press the slices down firmly before sealing so the brine holds them submerged.

content.text: Bread and butter pickles belong on a burger, on the side of a pulled pork sandwich, or inside a grilled cheese. They are the standard American pickle: sweet, sharp, crunchy. The name points to their Depression-era origins as a thrifty filling for plain bread. The same brine works with courgette slices or green beans.
